expectations.text: Applies the deletion test by name (as a named heading or explicit step)
expectations.evidence: Has '## Deletion test' heading and walks through what happens if the interface is deleted.

expectations.text: States the two-adapter rule (one adapter = hypothetical/indirection, two = real seam)
expectations.evidence: 'A **seam** is real only when at least two adapters live behind it. - You have one adapter... One adapter = indirection.'

expectations.text: Recommends deletion (conditional on real test-double adapter is acceptable)
expectations.evidence: 'Yes. Rip it out.' with sound conditional: keep only if hand-written FakePaymentGatewayClient exists.

expectations.text: Uses at least 3 of the skill vocabulary terms
expectations.evidence: Uses seam, adapter, port, leverage, depth differential, hypothetical seam — well above 3.
'Your junior's argument is the classic anti-pattern... textbook version of adding a port for one implementation.'

expectations.text: Does NOT propose new interface signatures or implementation code
expectations.evidence: No code proposals for new interfaces; only diagnosis and the 'discuss before designing' candidate.

expectations.text: Ends with 'Which of these would you like to explore?' or equivalent
expectations.evidence: Final line is exactly 'Which of these would you like to explore?'
